rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RICHARD P. GRANZELLA TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For (1) Instructions Regarding Annuity Payments During Trust Litigation And (2) Instructions Regarding Interests In Prime Smoked Meats, Inc. And Related Litigation
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) was not served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before this hearing. The judicial officer is to review and determine: 1) Before instructing petitioner as trustee, does the Court need additional information re Lillian Granzella-Boyles, the settlor's ex-spouse and income beneficiary under the trust amendment challenged by David Granzella's petition? It is unclear whether anyone has met with Lillian to observe her legal capacity to make decisions and her understanding of this case presenting a trust contest that may jeopardize her right to receive $48,000 per year during her life, and whether anyone is aware of Lillian's financial circumstances (would the Court's instruction to the trustee to withhold any distribution to her until the trust contest is resolved impose an economic hardship on her?). 2) Should the Court deny instructing petitioner as trustee as to whether he should substitute in the civil litigation pending in Contra Costa County as moot, as the Contra Costa Court has already granted petitioner's substitution? 3) Should the Court instruct petitioner as trustee to move for stay of the civil litigation until the trust contest is resolved in this case, whether by settlement or trial? i) If the trust amendment is invalid, the 2008 trust would become the operative instrument, and Steven Sacks (civil defendant and cross-complainant) would become a beneficiary to receive the trust's interest in Prime Smoked Meats, in which case should the trustee be instructed to abandon the litigation? ii) If the trust amendment is valid, should the trustee be instructed to defend Steven's cross-complaint purporting to enforce the settlor's agreement to distribute his interest in Prime Smoked Meats from the trust to Steven after the settlor's death, as the cross-complaint may be time-barred under CCP section 366.3 requiring an action to enforce "a claim that arises from a promise or agreement with a decedent to distribution from an estate or trust" to be filed within one year after the date of death? 3) Should the Court authorize petitioner as trustee to use the trust assets to prosecute and defend the civil litigation, as authorized by Article 7.2(g)(xv) of the trust amendment (and also by Article 14-3(z)of the 2008 trust in the event the trust amendment fails)? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TINA MEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been cleared: (1) Disclaimer is irrevocable and not an assignment, disposing of the disclaimed interests as if the disclaimant predeceased the decedent. Prob. Code sections. 281. Petitioner alleges that Ms. A. Mei has two children who are entitled to her share as a result of the disclaimer. It appears that the petition needs to be properly amended to mirror the said proposed distribution (to Ms. A. Mei's issues, considering one issue is a minor), or the petitioner needs to provide an authority under which the court can grant the proposed distribution of Ms. A. Mei's shares to the petitioner, not her children. (2) The children of the disclaimant must be properly noticed. (3) Assets included in the Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) must be identified to ensure that all accounts are accurately listed on Attachment 1. If any account contains securities held in a brokerage account, these must be appraised by the probate referee. Listing merely the names of the financial institutions is insufficient. In such cases, a new probate referee must be appointed, and a corrected Inventory and Appraisal must be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DORIS JOHANNA MEHLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/10/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) Petition is incomplete and inconsistent: the caption does not indicate that IAEA authority is requested, although Item 2c requests full authority. In Item 5a, box 3 or 4 must be checked, and box 7 or 8 must be checked. Item 6 is blank, although it appears to apply. Item 7 is blank, although it appears to apply. If a will names a trust or trustee as a devisee, the trustee must be clearly identified in item 8 . Where the personal representative and the sole trustee are the same person, or if there is no trustee, the beneficiaries of the trust must be clearly identified (Probate Code Section 1208(b) and San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.C.2). Petitioner's name and relationship to decedent must be listed in Item 8. The instant petition indicates that the named executors have declined to serve, but no declinations have been filed. 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. 4) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 5) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). 6) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 02/02/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER J. PAVLOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to the next available date (2/18/2026). The petition appears premature for examiner review, as only the petition and proof of publication have been filed. A proposed order must be submitted, including a copy of the lost will being offered to be admitted. A copy of the lost will being offered to probate must be submitted separately. LRSF 14.20. All required documents must be filed two weeks before the hearing.
